publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-07-05 00:13:00 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: The Janet Hamill Archive contains extensive materials that document the life and work of Janet Hamill and illuminates Hamill's unique relationship with her longtime friend Patti Smith.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The Janet Hamill Archive contains extensive materials that document the life and work of Janet Hamill. The archive additionally illuminates Hamill's unique relationship with her longtime friend, poet, writer, singer-songwriter and artist Patti Smith. Included are correspondence (approximately 380 letters), manuscripts, journals, notebooks, diaries (several thousand pages dated 1967-2007) and photographs documenting Hamill's work. Also included are materials pertaining to Patti Smith consisting of correspondence (approximately 100 letters), manuscripts and notes concerning the book Just Kids (approximately 1,000 pages) and photographs of and by Patti Smith.

emph.ref: Series II: Writings by Janet Hamill
Series III: Announcements for Performances/Readings and Related Material
Series V: Audio and Visual Items
Series VI: Journals, Diaries and Notebooks
Series VIII: Personal Effects and Miscellaneous Items

p.ref: Subseries Xa: Correspondence with Janet Hamill
Subseries Xb: Photographs of Patti Smith
Subseries Xc: Artwork by Patti Smith
Subseries Xd: Just Kids Manuscripts and Notes
Subseries Xg: Performances and Events with Patti Smith and Janet Hamill

list.head: Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
list.item: Fleurs du Mal: in Pattern and Prose, by Charles Baudelaire
The Letters of Charles Baudelaire to his Mother, 1833-1866, by Charles Baudelaire
Genioy Figura de Jorge Luis Borges , by Alicia Jurado

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Janet Hamill archive, #8062. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Poets, American -- New York (State) -- New York.
Artists, American -- New York (State) -- New York.
controlaccess.persname: Smith, Patti. (Title of work: Just kids..)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 donation envelope; 1 information pamphlet; 1 free gift flyer; 1 addressed and stamped return envelope.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American composer, musician, conductor, and writer. Amram has composed more than 100 orchestral and chamber music works, and written many scores for Broadway theater and film, including Robert Frank and Alfred Leslie's Pull My Daisy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anderson is the manager of the Strand Rare Book Room. He is the stepson of American playwright Maxwell Anderson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American author. Auster has published poems, essays, novels of his own as well as translations of French writers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Baron is a photographer and collaborated on a book with Janet Hamill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Musician and performer, best known for being a member of the seminal New York No Wave band The Contortions and part of the early all-girl band The Bloods. Had roles in many New York Underground films and is also known for her work as a backup singer. She and Janet are longtime friends and performed together in the past

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poet and performer, he and Janet had numerous readings together

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Former Janet English professor and chair of English Department at Rowan University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cherry was the editor at Norton for the never realized Eternal Café, a collaboration between Janet and Paula Smith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writer, editor, poet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cathy Cress attended Glassboro State College with Janet and was the editor of the college newspaper. She has an MSW in aging and is an expert on geriatric care.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: DeRugeris attended Glassboro State College with Janet. He is a poet and author of speculative fiction

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopy of article by Janet's father

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hanuman Books publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Janet's sister-in-law.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Janet's sister.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Janet's sister.

did.physdesc: 3 ALS, 5 APCS, 6 EM, 1 event, 1 MS, 2 TLS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holman was instrumental in the reopening of the Nuyorican Poets Café and founded the Bowery Poetry Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographer and artist. Hudson is a longtime friend of both Patti Smith and Janet.

did.physdesc: 3 ACS, 8 ALS, 14 APCS, 2 MS, 2 TLS, 1 Photo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barbara Kruger is a well-known conceptual artist and was friends with JH throughout the 70's. They were introduced to each other by Ken Tisa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gloryia Okulski was an inspirational English teacher during JH's junior year of high school. Janet considers her responsible for her being a poet

did.physdesc: 4 ACS, 12 APCS, 1 EM, 4 ALS, 1 fax, 8 TLS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American poet, publisher and editor. Owen was co-director of the St. Mark's Poetry Project in New York City. She is the editor and publisher of Telephone Books and has published two of JH's books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "The New Romantics--A Manifesto."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An artist and early friend of Janet and Patti Smith. He has also collaborated with poets Max Blagg and Kenward Elmslie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Played bass in Janet's band.

did.physdesc: 3 ACS, 2 APCS, 1 ALS, 9 Events, 2 TLS, 6 photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Janet's former English professor and friend.

did.physdesc: 8 ACS, 2 ALS, 8 APCS, 1 EM, 4 Events, MS, 11 Misc.

did.unittitle: Series II: Writings by Janet Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proofs with corrections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bowery Books, related correspondence, publishing information, photographs, promotional material, guest book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Self-published chapbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of the manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Emails, notebooks, photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, small journal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, including Ocean View Books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Art boards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Final proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: MS drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and notebooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Self-published chapbook and front layout and artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Telephone books, transcripts, manuscript pages, copies of manuscript, book mock-up, photographs, certificate of copyright registration

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letterpress printed in an edition of 100 by Oliphant Press

did.unittitle: "The Big Sleep", written under Juan Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 copies of the broadside. 1 copy has the name "Juan" crossed out and replaced by "Janet." Also included is the layout, with original artwork, for the broadside.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed, no. 3 of 50

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Broadside and layout.

did.unittitle: "From 'The Book of Marvels'", written under Juan Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 47. Also included is the layout, with original artwork, for the broadside.

did.unittitle: "Madame Bogart's Arts of Animal Ecstasy", written under Juan Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed, no. 99. Also included is the layout for the broadside.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also included is the layout, with original artwork, for the broadside.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 84

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 issues. Glassboro State College literary magazine. Both Patti (Patricia) Smith and Janet Hamill were on the Avant staff. It served as an early area over which the two bonded. Work by JH, but not PS, appears in several of these issues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted poetry manuscripts, "Delouz Entago", drafts, "A Poem for All Seasons", handwritten lists.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder with assorted items, including: proofs for "The Birds of the Air" included in the Gary Indiana anthology Living with the Animals, also included is some correspondence with Indiana and the book's editor; tear sheet from Kansas Quarterly; and items related to Janet's contribution to More Reflections on the Meaning of Life.

did.unittitle: Subseries IIf: Other Material on Janet Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted newspapers, writings and a student paper on JH.

did.unittitle: Series III: Announcements for Performances/Readings and Related Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assorted materials, including correspondence, books, brochures, receipts, and travel material related to Janet's trip to the UK to present a reading and workshop, also included is some correspondence related to Janet's UK trip to participate in the Latitude Festival.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 rubber stamps used for flyers and announcement cards, including readings at The Poetry Project 1978? and a reading with Max Blagg at the Ear Inn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 ½ x 14 inch poster for JH reading with Max Blagg at St. Marks Church. The poster has poems by both Blagg and JH. Also included is the layout, with original artwork by Kenneth Tisa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 ½ by 14 inch poster for reading by Juan Hamill (JH's nom de plume) at 41 Worth Street. The poster has a poem by JH. Also included is the layout, with original artwork by Kenneth Tisa.

did.unittitle: Ear Inn reading with Ravi Singh

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcard, source material and layout for the postcard.

did.unittitle: Short Pieces: Electric Music, Performance, Film

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JH performed as "Janet H." at 33 Grand Street. Other performers, included Laurie Anderson, Adele Bertei, Rhys Chatham, Glenn Branca, John Lurie, Jeffrey Lohn, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 9 x 14 inch posters for reading that also included Maxwell Blagg, David Matlin, and others. On the verso of one of the posters is a manuscript, with corrections, of JH poem "The Two Yellow Pills."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small flyer for a reading that included Juan Hamill (JH's nom de plume) at A.I.R. Gallery. Also included is the layout with original artwork for the flyer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyer and original artwork for JH reading with Adele Bertei and Max Blagg at the Mud[d Club].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyer for the festival in which JH participated. There is also correspondence concerning a possible JH reading at the Strand Bookstore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 flyers for JH and Moving Star's performance as part of Whitman Sunday at the Walt Whitman Cultural Arts Center, Camden N.J. There is also some correspondence concerning the event.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Schedule and poster for the event, Mar 30–Apr 1, 2001. Patti Smith and her Band played with JH and Moving Star. The Gathering featured Stanley Kunitz, John Ashbery, Brenda Hillman, Galway Kinnell, and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JH participated in this Rededication of The Cathedral Church of Saint John the Devine. Folder includes invitation to the event, correspondence, stage pass, t-shirt, and other items. Other participants included, among many, Mike Albo, Samantha Jones, and Jayne Cortez.

did.unittitle: Word: The Jay Liveson Memorial Poetry and Music Festival

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JH participated in this festival presented by Exoterica and The College of Mount Saint Vincent (Bronx, N.Y). Folder includes flyer and card for the event, correspondence and program. Other participants included, among many, Billy Collins, Jim Carroll, Richard Hell, Edwin Torres, and Big Youth.

did.unittitle: A Poet Paints: A One Woman Show

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyer, publicity photo, statement, checklist (with prices), and press coverage of JH solo exhibition of her paintings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 30 assorted items for JH readings and performances, including: participation in Stan Vanderbeek's New Wilderness event "Dream Fest" , Sep 17, 1979; reading with Ed Friedman at the Zu; "All Hallow's Eve" reading with Annabel Levitt at St. Mark's Church; reading with Maureen Owen at the Ear Inn; reading with Jack Collom at St. Mark's Church; reading (as Juan Hamill) with Barbara Kruger at Artists Space; and programs from The Poetry Project's Annual New Year's Day Marathon Reading.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 50 assorted items, primarily flyers and "set lists" for JH readings and performances, including: participation in "Greatest Hits 60 Poets: 60 Poems All-Star Benefit" for NYC Poetry Calendar at Environ ; original artwork for flyer for "Ladies Choice" reading with Ann Powell and Ruth Kligman at 11 West 20; items related to participation in "Praise Day for Philip Lamatia! 'Becoming Visible'"; readings 17 at the 9th Precinct Gallery, ABC NO RIO and the Figaro Cafe; and flyer and program for "Jet of Blood" directed by Bob Holman at Ward Nasse Gallery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 items, including posters announcing: The Poetry Project Valentine's Day Reading, 1988 ; Rose Drachler Memorial Reading, 1983, and "Poems at the 9th Precinct Gallery".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 15 items, including posters announcing: The Poets Theatre and The Poetry Project's Annual New Year's Day Marathon Reading. Programs include the program for "Patti Smith's Meltdown," London, 2005 at which JH also performed. Also: posters for "The Blank Generation and Beyond," grand opening of the Pioneer Theater, New York.

did.unittitle: The Poets Theatre and Kerouac posters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20 3 ¼ x 4 ¼ inch black and white Polaroid photographs taken by JH on her Moroccan trip.

did.unittitle: Morocco photographs taken by Neil Winokur

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 black and white photographs, of assorted sizes, of Morocco and JH in Morocco. There are also copy negatives for one of the photographs and a contact sheet of the negatives.

did.unittitle: Brion Gysin and Adele Bertei at the Nova Convention

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 x 10 inch gelatin silver photograph by Marcia Resnick.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 x 10 inch copy photograph from the Museum of Natural History.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three 8 x 10 inch black and white photographs by Neil Winokur.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 38 2 ¾ x 3 ½ inch photographs of JH and friends on a road trip.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 small black and white and color photographs of various sizes, including travel shots of JH and friends, 72 color snapshots including many photos of JH artwork, 44 black and white snapshots, largely of JH's home studio, Approx. 10 photographs of various sizes, including publicity photos.

did.physdesc: 11 x 14 inch gelatin silver photograph. Signed by the photographer Judy Linn on verso.

did.unittitle: Series V: Audio and Visual items
Bryan's Genie of the Alphabet visuals

dao.xlink:title: Bryan's Genie of the Alphabet visuals

daodesc.p: Bryan's Genie of the Alphabet visuals

did.unittitle: Spoken Words live, Patti Smith group live, Moving Star live
Democracy Now! live, The Poetry Project live, the Poetry Project's 28th Annual New Year's Day Reading, Moving Star
JH reading at Stockton State College, shot by Joel Sternfeld, ½ inch video
Cyber Forest, Janet Hamill with Shrubs
Resist to be with the living
Patti Smith, Janet Hamill, Oliver Ray
2 cassette tapes of JH reading, one marked St. Marks.
Janet Hamill with Bob Torsello and Jay LoRubbio
Janet Hamill, Moving Star, Patti Smith rough mixes
Series VI: Journals, Diaries and Notebooks
Notebook: Temple of Destruction, Mme. Bogart
Notebook with sketches from Ken Tisa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes on Subterraneous, Trans Atlantic and the Other Side, Seven Veils, Spell Bound, Flying Nowhere and Millennium

did.unittitle: Notebook with correspondence, photographs, and clippings
Janet Hamill and Moving Star flyers
Series VIII: Personal Effects and Miscellaneous Items
Goshen Public Library Poetry materials, including photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 original ink drawings, signed by the artist. Sizes vary, approx.5 ½ x 7 inches. 1 print of one of the ink drawings, 8 ½ x 11 inches. Color drawing of a pyramid shape with collaged color snapshot of pink pelicans, titled "Paradise." Signed and inscribed by the artist, approx. 8 x 11 inches

did.unittitle: Joel Sternfeld "Bar/Man in Hat/Young Girl's Hair"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color photograph of inside a bar in the "Deep South", 15 ¾ x 11 ½ inches in a 20 x 16 inch mat. Both the photo and mat are soiled. Signed by the artist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "reading moonlight or reading on the moon" broadside, signed and inscribed by the poet, 12 x 16 inches.

did.unittitle: Subseries Xa: Correspondence with Janet Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written on cover: "Featuring old correspondence Note: new correspondence can be designated by blue fountain pen," return address at the Hotel Chelsea, and "Patricia L Smith." There are also several stickers on the cover. Inside are handwritten poems, thoughts, songs, and lists written to JH. Items laid in at different pages include: two poems, "exclusive pix of Howie [Howard Michaels]," ad for Jesse Winchester album, and clipping of James Dean on newsprint.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs, poems, and a lock of Janet's brother's hair.

did.unittitle: Flyer for Gerard Malanga and Smith reading
Letter on stationary from the White House, London
Piece of cloth with "Chelsea" written on it with envelope
Clipping from The Herald on Female Super 8

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written on the back of the typescript of "Piano," a Smith poem, with some handwriting also on it. The envelope has a sticker of a duck.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written on the back of a photocopy of the corrected cover layout for PS's Witt, published by Gotham Book Mart, 1973. There is also some writing on the photocopy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written on "Correograma" stationery and sent from Mexico.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Polaroid photograph of Janet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With photograph of "Smith Kitchen"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With photograph of Smith children.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many include children's scribbles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Gale" manuscript, clippings from papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With manuscript of "February Snow".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With manuscript of "The Coral Sea" and "The Writer's Song"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 copies of the Memorial Service announcement card for Fred "Sonic" Smith,and 2 copies of the Mariners' Church of Detroit Memorial Service program. Includes some photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With memorial cards.

did.unittitle: Napkin with note to Joe Csida

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes laminated memorial card for Beverly Williams Smith, PS's mother; black ribbon; and folded memorial card.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts of poems that became Auguries of Innocence, 2005, 53 pp. Many corrections, in the hand of JH, throughout.

did.unittitle: Subseries Xb: Photographs of Patti Smith
Subseries Xc: Artwork by Patti Smith
Photographs, some for Body of Water

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A 4 7/8 x 7 ½ inch wood (cigar?) box that PS created after visiting Federico García Lorca's niece and handling an "original" copy of Poeta en Nueva York. PS presented the box to JH. It contains items that she brought back from Spain and includes: linen gloves that PS wore while handling the García Lorca manuscript, an unburned votive candle; a dried flower; a picture of García Lorca; a paper lace doily; a British pound coin; a Spanish postcard from 1903 of an unidentified woman; and a slip of paper with "Octubre 2007" printed on it.

did.unittitle: Subseries Xd: Just Kids Manuscripts and Notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inserted in back of pad is handwritten manuscript on notebook graph paper of the poem "Wild Leaves" that appears only in the paperback edition of Just Kids. Also inserted in back are a color photocopy of six SX-70 photographs of Jesse Smith (?) in a graveyard with Smith family gravestones and a photocopy from parts of a Los Angeles Times article about Robert Mapplethorpe's artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including chronologies, journal entries, letters (never sent), dreams and poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, questions, suggestions for sections and areas needing to be developed, including tone and focus of the book. Also noted are new sections, additional writing and other changes that have been done to the manuscript.

did.unittitle: Hotel Chelsea folder with typed manuscript pages
"Separate Ways Together" with notes and writings
"East Side Sutra: View from a Window", The Press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A complete issue of The Press, a monthly New York City newspaper. It contains a section "The East Village: The Pangs of Rebirth," with this Patti Smith contributed article.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With an issue of Vanity Fair

did.unittitle: "Return of Patti Smith", The Village Voice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Smith's reed from when she played with JH and Moving Star

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Announcement card for Patti Smith solo exhibition at the Robert Miller Gallery. The exhibition included recent photographs as well as an installation piece, a composite of objects, drawings, and a vintage silk cloth inscribed by the arti

did.unittitle: Just Kids excerpt in Rolling Stone
Forward to Nostalgia of the Infinite
Postcard of Jean-Paul Belmondo sent to Janet Hamill
Photograph of Hamill and Smith taken at the Village Gate
Framed self-portrait of Smith and Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drawing with colored pencil of PS with JH, 1966. This was made while JH was living with PS's family. It is inscribed by PS: "Chaps, ltd. 1966. With love to Chaps." According to JH, this drawing had been in the possession of Robert Mapplethorpe for over twenty years. It was given back to PS, by his estate, after his death. PS later gave the drawing to JH. 10 x 13 drawing in 19 x 23 inch frame.

did.unittitle: Memorial card with Patti Smith poem
List of speakers at Mapplethorpe memorial
Invitation to a ceremony commemorating the anniversary of Mapplethorpe's cancelled exhibit
Subseries Xg: Performances and Events with Patti Smith and Janet Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handbill, for a reading with Anne Powell, Juan Hamill (Janet Hamill) and Patti Smith at the Razor Gallery. Also included is the original collage artwork for the handbill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JH read her poems as the opening act for Patti Smith's Central Park Summerstage appearance. Included are: an artist's agreement, posters, t-shirt, Neil Strauss's New York Times review, clippings, and poems with handwritten notes that JH read.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JH and Patti Smith were brought to Boston by their old friend Patti Hudson. This was Smith's first Boston appearance in 16 years. They did an evening of readings together and had an exhibition. Smith showed Polaroids at the Art Market Gallery, and JH showed her watercolors. Included are: publicity around the events, some correspondence and a t-shirt made for the event.

did.unittitle: Liss Ard Festival, Co. Cork, Ireland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Patti Smith was the festival's first director in 1997. JH participated in the festival and did several readings, including a reading with Patti Smith and Nick Cave. Includes: photographs of the event (approx 70 color snapshots and 11 8 x 10 inch black and white photographs); itinerary; correspondence; tshirts; "The Gardens Voice: Liss Ard 97", and press folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JH and Moving Star opened for Patti Smith and her Band. Includes: participant badge; letter of agreement; some correspondence; and programs for the event.

did.unittitle: Strange Messenger, opening party and reading

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyer for JH and Moving Star performance at the Warhol Museum. At the time "Strange Messenger: The Art of Patti Smith" was on also display at the Museum. Also included in the folder is JH agreement with the Museum, correspondence and other items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 2005 Meltdown was directed by Patti Smith. She performed along with many others, including JH, Antony and the Johnsons, Beth Orton, Billy Bragg, Cat Power, Joanna Newsom, John Cale, Lenny Kaye, Martha Wainwright, Marc Almond, Richard Hell, Roy Harper, Sinéad O'Connor, Television, and Yoko Ono. JH performed with John Giorno and also discussed her work with Smith. Included are: the event's program, stage pass, correspondence, letter of agreement, publicity, and 2 handwritten manuscripts in JH's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JH and Moving Star were the opening act for Patti Smith's 2005 Central Park Summerstage appearance. Included are: stage pass, correspondence, set list and 34 snapshots of the event.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Both Patti Smith and JH performed at the first annual Latitude Festival. Included are a UK newspaper with an article about the festival and 2 performer wristbands.

did.unittitle: An Intimate Evening with Patti Smith and Janet Hamill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Both Smith and JH attended Rowan when it was named Glassboro State College. Included are: program, set list, press release, and photocopies of snapshots.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arista Records, Test Pressing, Demonstration Copy, Not for Sale. From the Wartoke Concern, Inc

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arista Records; initial release without sleeve. From the Wartoke Concern, Inc

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arista Records, Promotional Copy, Not for Sale. From the Wartoke Concern, Inc

did.unittitle: Test pressing for Velvet Underground album "The Velvet Underground and Nico"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the Wartoke Concern, Inc

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arista Records, Demonstration Copy, Not for Sale; in original sleeve; cover photo by Judy Linn. Liner notes signed and dated by Patti Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arista Records, original release in original sleeve; not inscribed; cover photo by Lynn Goldsmith. Liner notes signed by PS and also with the mark of a cross in her hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arista Records, loaned for promotion only, not for sale; in original sleeve; not inscribed; cover photo by Robert Mapplethorpe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arista Records; original release in original sleeve; not inscribed; cover photo by Robert Mapplethorpe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sire Records, Special Collector's Edition; sleeve only; inscribed; cover photo Sandy Daley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With Janet Hamill, Lenny Kaye, and Kimberly Smith, signed by all of the performers, 14 x 22 inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Patti Smith and Special Guests, Nick Cave, Donal Dineen, David Gray, Janet Hamill, Ron Sexsmith, Liss Ard Festival, Co. Cork, Ireland, Sep 3–7, 1997, 33 x 23 ½ inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letterpress printed by Leslie Miller at The Grenfell Press for Hanuman Books, 12 ½ x 15 inches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letterpress printed by Leslie Miller at The Grenfell Press for Hanuman Books.
